perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “ BCSI ” shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished; and (ii) all reports, summaries, compilations, analyses, notes or other information which contain such information. Written information containing BCSI furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confide ntial BCSI .”
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “ BCSI ” shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO and explicitly marked as such, furnished after the date hereof, in written form only; and (ii) only final reports approved by VELCO which directly quote marked BCSI, excluding any analysis or summaries. Written information containing BCSI furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confide ntial BCSI .”
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity into the definition of BCSI. Originally, BCSI included all information designated by VELCO, regardless of format or timing. The modified version limits BCSI to information explicitly marked and furnished after the agreement date and includes ONLY final reports approved by VELCO and that directly quote marked BCSI, excluding other derivatives or analysis. This contradicts the original broader definition. This contradicts Section IV, paragraph 2 and 3, which discusses protecting the Classified Information in accordance with designation, because not all information that could be BCSI is considered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information shall be marked and designated in accordance with CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT VELCO Non-Disclosure Agreement – Rev 0 – Dec 21 2015 3 Sections I, II, and/or III above, and Recipient shall protect the Classified Information in accordance with that designation. 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO .
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of BCSI, limiting it to explicitly marked, written information furnished *after* the agreement date and *only* to final approved reports that directly quote marked BCSI. This directly contradicts the contradicted text in Section IV, paragraphs 2 and 3, which requires the recipient to protect *all* Classified Information in accordance with its designation, regardless of the format, timing, or whether it's in a final report or analysis. The original text included all information designated by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished. The change now excludes certain information that was originally considered BCSI, making it unclear which information needs to be protected under Section IV. The restriction on BCSI to post-agreement information and approved final reports directly conflicts with the requirement to protect a broader set of Classified Information as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, CEII shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished; and ( ii) all reports, summaries, compilations, analyses, notes or other information which contain such information. Written information containing CEII that is furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confidential CEII ”.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, CEII shall mean: (i) only that information explicitly designated as CEII in writing by VELCO and furnished after the effective date of this agreement; and (ii) solely final, signed engineering reports containing CEII, excluding any drafts, summaries, or preliminary findings. Written information containing CEII that is furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confidential CEII ”.
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the definition of CEII significantly. Previously, it included all information designated as such by VELCO, regardless of format or timing. Now, it only includes information explicitly designated in writing after the effective date and only final, signed engineering reports, excluding other forms of documentation. This creates an **in-text contradiction** because other sections (like Section IV regarding protection of information) assume a broader definition of CEII. If the CEII is not in engineering reports, it is not consider CEII.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information shall be marked and designated in accordance with CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT VELCO Non-Disclosure Agreement – Rev 0 – Dec 21 2015 3 Sections I, II, and/or III above, and Recipient shall protect the Classified Information in accordance with that designation. 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO .
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of CEII, limiting it to explicitly designated written information after the agreement's effective date and only final, signed engineering reports. This contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to 'Classified Information' (which, based on the explanation, relies on the broader definition of CEII), and imposes obligations on how to handle notes and other information that might contain CEII. Because the changed text limits CEII only to signed engineering reports, any notes or other forms of Classified Information is no longer considered CEII, thus creating a contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO prior to disclosure to Recipient . It includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential . CI subject to this Agreement may be in intangible form, such as information communicated orally or by visual observation, or may be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean only information specifically marked "Confidential" in writing by VELCO at the time of disclosure to Recipient. It excludes any information received orally, visually, or any information regarding third parties, regardless of VELCO’s obligations. CI subject to this Agreement is exclusively in tangible document form, specifically limited to final reports. The term "document" includes only final reports.
perturbation.explanation: The revised definition of CI now drastically restricts its scope. Initially, CI included a broad range of confidential information, including that of third parties and information in intangible form. The revised definition limits CI to explicitly marked written information, excluding oral and visual communications and third-party information. This is a clear **in-text contradiction** with the agreement's broader stipulations for confidentiality found in Section IV, since those stipulations are applicable to a definition of CI broader than the new perturbed definition, because the section is still reffering to the older definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information shall be marked and designated in accordance with CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT VELCO Non-Disclosure Agreement – Rev 0 – Dec 21 2015 3 Sections I, II, and/or III above, and Recipient shall protect the Classified Information in accordance with that designation. 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO .
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'CI' to only explicitly marked written information, excluding oral/visual communications and third-party information. The contradicted text outlines recipient obligations to protect 'Classified Information' which includes a broader range of information based on the original definition of 'CI'. Because the change reduces the scope of CI, the obligations of the contradicted text can no longer be fulfilled. This constitutes a direct contradiction because the obligation outlined in the contradicted text now relies on a prior definition of CI no longer in effect.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall use their best efforts, but is not legally bound, to maintain the confidentiality of the Classified Information. The Recipient may divulge Classified Information to any third party at any time, as long as they verbally notify VELCO after the fact. The Recipient is explicitly not responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces significant ambiguity and **in-text contradiction** by weakening the confidentiality obligations and removing liability for representative breaches. Initially, the Recipient was legally obligated to maintain confidentiality and was responsible for representative breaches. Now, they are only expected to use 'best efforts' and can disclose information to third parties with only a verbal notification afterward. This contradicts the essence of the NDA, as other sections rely on the assumption of strict confidentiality and responsibility of the recipient. Specifically, this creates an **in-text contradiction** with Section X, where it says that "Recipient agrees that any breach of this Agreement would cause VELCO substantial and irreparable harm and, therefore, in the event of any such breach or threatened breach, in addition to other remedies which may be available, VELCO shall have the right to specific performance and other injunctive and equitable relief, it being acknowledged that legal remed ies are inadequate." because, according to this change, there is not a breach, therefore no irreparable harm can happen.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Equitable Relief; Audit. The provisions of this Agreement are necessary for the protection of the business and goodwill of the parties and are considered by the parties to be reasonable for such purpose . Recipient agrees that any breach of this Agreement would cause VELCO substantial and irreparable harm and, therefore, in the event of any such breach or threatened breach, in addition to other remedies which may be available, VELCO shall have the right to specific performance and other injunctive and equitable relief, it being acknowledged that legal remed ies are inadequate. VELCO may audit the Recipient ’s compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens confidentiality obligations and removes liability for representative breaches. The original text held the Recipient legally obligated to maintain confidentiality and responsible for representative breaches. The changed text states they only need to use 'best efforts' and can disclose information to third parties with verbal notification. This directly contradicts Section 10, which states that any breach of the Agreement would cause irreparable harm, as the changed text significantly reduces the likelihood of a breach (or even defines what constitutes a breach). Therefore, there is a direct and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that VELCO, in its sole discre tion, so requests , the Recipient will promptly return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , all Classified Information, including all copies, reproductions, compilations, analyses or extracts thereof, whether electronic or paper media.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that VELCO requests, the Recipient may, at their discretion, return to VELCO any Classified Information still readily available, but is not required to destroy or account for any copies, reproductions, or extracts thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by making the return of Classified Information optional for the Recipient. The original text mandates prompt return or destruction of all Classified Information upon VELCO's request. The modified text allows the Recipient to return only 'readily available' information at their discretion, eliminating the requirement to destroy or account for copies. This contradicts the underlying purpose of the agreement, which is to protect Classified Information and ensures it does not remain in the possession of the Recipient indefinitely, making Section XI meaningless, creating an **in-text contradiction**.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Survival . The Recipient/ remains bound by these provisions unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes the return of Classified Information optional for the Recipient, contradicting the original text which mandates prompt return or destruction of all Classified Information upon VELCO's request. This alteration directly clashes with Section 11 ('Survival'), which states that the Recipient remains bound by the provisions unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation. The changed text weakens this obligation, making Section 11 less enforceable and creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that any breach of this Agreement would cause VELCO substantial and irreparable harm and, therefore, in the event of any such breach or threatened breach, in addition to other remedies which may be available, VELCO shall have the right to specific performance and other injunctive and equitable relief, it being acknowledged that legal remed ies are inadequate. VELCO may audit the Recipient ’s compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that a breach of this Agreement may cause VELCO some harm. However, VELCO's sole remedy for any breach shall be limited to monetary damages, not to exceed $100. VELCO shall not have the right to audit the Recipient's compliance.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the enforcement provisions. Initially, VELCO had the right to specific performance, injunctive relief, and could audit the Recipient. The modified text limits VELCO's remedy to monetary damages capped at $100 and eliminates the right to audit. This change introduces ambiguity around the recourse VELCO has if its confidential information is compromised. This contradicts Section XI, the part about the agreement surviving, creating an **in-text contradiction**, because the recipient is acknowledging the breach only may cause harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Survival . The Recipient/ remains bound by these provisions unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed VELCO specific performance, injunctive relief, and the ability to audit the Recipient. The changed text limits VELCO's remedy to monetary damages capped at $100 and removes the right to audit, which directly contradicts the original enforcement provisions and introduces ambiguity. This contradicts Section 11, which states the Recipient remains bound by the provisions unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation, because the modified text acknowledges the breach may only cause harm, which contradicts the clause that states the recipient remains bound unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
